# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis Master-Slave Replication Configuration
# High-Availability Cache Cluster Setup

# ==========================================
# Master Node Configuration
# ==========================================
master:
  # Network settings
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  port: 6379
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  supervised: "no"
  pidfile: /var/run/redis/redis-master.pid
  loglevel: notice
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-master.log
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting / Persistence
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: dump-master.rdb
  dir: /var/lib/redis/master

  # Replication - Master specific settings
  # No authentication required for slave connections (simplified setup)
  requirepass: ""
  masterauth: ""

  # Minimum number of slaves that must be connected for master to accept writes
  # Set to 0 so writes succeed even if all slaves are down
  min-slaves-to-write: 0
  # Maximum lag in seconds for a slave to be considered healthy
  min-slaves-max-lag: 10

  # Replication backlog settings
  repl-backlog-size: 1mb
  repl-backlog-ttl: 3600
  repl-diskless-sync: "no"
  repl-diskless-sync-delay: 5
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"

  # Memory management
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Append Only File (AOF) persistence
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: appendonly-master.aof
  appendfsync: everysec
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: 64mb

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

  # Client output buffer limits for slaves
  client-output-buffer-limit:
    normal: 0 0 0
    slave: 256mb 64mb 60
    pubsub: 32mb 8mb 60

# ==========================================
# Slave Node 1 Configuration
# ==========================================
slave1:
  # Network settings
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  port: 6380
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  supervised: "no"
  pidfile: /var/run/redis/redis-slave1.pid
  loglevel: notice
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-slave1.log
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting / Persistence
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: dump-slave1.rdb
  dir: /var/lib/redis/slave1

  # Replication - Slave specific settings
  # Point to the master node
  slaveof:
    master-host: redis-master
    master-port: 6379

  # No authentication needed to connect to master
  masterauth: ""
  requirepass: ""

  # Slave is read-only by default for data safety
  slave-read-only: "yes"
  slave-serve-stale-data: "yes"
  slave-priority: 100

  # Replication settings
  repl-diskless-sync: "no"
  repl-diskless-sync-delay: 5
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"

  # Memory management
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Append Only File (AOF) persistence
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: appendonly-slave1.aof
  appendfsync: everysec
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: 64mb

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

  # Client output buffer limits
  client-output-buffer-limit:
    normal: 0 0 0
    slave: 256mb 64mb 60
    pubsub: 32mb 8mb 60

# ==========================================
# Slave Node 2 Configuration
# ==========================================
slave2:
  # Network settings
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  port: 6381
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  supervised: "no"
  pidfile: /var/run/redis/redis-slave2.pid
  loglevel: notice
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-slave2.log
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting / Persistence
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: dump-slave2.rdb
  dir: /var/lib/redis/slave2

  # Replication - Slave specific settings
  # Point to the master node
  slaveof:
    master-host: redis-master
    master-port: 6379

  # No authentication needed to connect to master
  masterauth: ""
  requirepass: ""

  # Slave is read-only by default for data safety
  slave-read-only: "yes"
  slave-serve-stale-data: "yes"
  # Higher priority means less likely to be promoted by Sentinel
  slave-priority: 101

  # Replication settings
  repl-diskless-sync: "no"
  repl-diskless-sync-delay: 5
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"

  # Memory management
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Append Only File (AOF) persistence
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: appendonly-slave2.aof
  appendfsync: everysec
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: 64mb

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

  # Client output buffer limits
  client-output-buffer-limit:
    normal: 0 0 0
    slave: 256mb 64mb 60
    pubsub: 32mb 8mb 60

# ==========================================
# Redis Sentinel Configuration
# For automatic failover and high availability
# ==========================================
sentinel:
  port: 26379
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  protected-mode: "no"
  logfile: /var/log/redis/sentinel.log
  dir: /var/lib/redis/sentinel

  # Monitor the master node
  # sentinel monitor <master-name> <ip> <port> <quorum>
  # Quorum of 2 means at least 2 sentinels must agree for failover
  monitor:
    master-name: cache-cluster-master
    host: redis-master
    port: 6379
    quorum: 2

  # Time in milliseconds before considering master as down
  down-after-milliseconds: 5000

  # Number of slaves that can be reconfigured simultaneously during failover
  parallel-syncs: 1

  # Failover timeout in milliseconds
  failover-timeout: 60000

  # No authentication for master
  auth-pass: ""

  # Notification and reconfig scripts (optional)
  # notification-script: /var/lib/redis/scripts/notify.sh
  # client-reconfig-script: /var/lib/redis/scripts/reconfig.sh
